preamble: A common historical shift where a voiced velar stop undergoes palatalization and subsequent affrication, particularly before front vowels.

examples.note: The geminate velar /gg/ palatalized and affricated to [dʒ].
Latin /g/ palatalized and affricated to [dʒ] before front vowels.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
